free_text: 198,199,200,201,202,204-Hg(n,g) cross sections and the
termination of s-process nucleosynthesis.
ORELA
Water moderated tantalum target
40-m flight path.
Pair of hexafluorobenzine (C6F6) scintillators
flux monitored by 6Li glass detector
Beam monitor.
Energy calibrated using well-known resonances in 27-Al
at 5.903 keV and 1.094 MeV.
Normalization constant determined by saturated
resonance technique using 4.9 eV resonance of 197Au
Data corrected for dead-time, background, neutrons
scattered in the sample, multiple scattering,
self-shielding, gamma absorptoin in the sample.
A detailed list of corrections and their uncertainties
is given in Table VIII.
R.L.Macklin, 86/4/9.
BIB update.
OS.
- Modified or added information on SAMPLE, STATUS,
CORRECTION, ERR-ANALYS.
- Corrected units in subs. 9-13
- Deleted subs. 19-23 (contradicting subs.14-18,
not strength function)
- Converted to lower case
- Added subs. 24-29 (Maxwellian averaged cs)
Enriched samples. HgO powder pressed to quadratic
tablets 26x26 mm, in 6.4 mu-m thick mylar bag.
Thickness=0.07 cm. Hg-196 content less than 0.05%.
-- Note by compiler: The isotopic composition as given
by authors in Table I amounts to only about 80%. --
Uncertainty from saturated resonance
calibration
Shape of Li-6(n,a) cross section
Pulse height weighting technique
